title: Reunion

jokes.text: your credit card called. They wanna make sure you're the one Buying cream soda in bulk.
jokes.setup: Credit card companies call about suspicious activity
jokes.punchline: The suspicious activity is buying cream soda in bulk
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail that reveals Liz's weird eating habits through mundane corporate security check

jokes.text: And your landlord called. And he said it is not the toilet, it's you.
jokes.setup: Landlord called about a plumbing issue
jokes.punchline: The problem isn't the toilet, it's Liz herself
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure - implies something gross about Liz without being explicit

jokes.text: That's his opinion.
jokes.setup: Landlord says the bathroom problem is Liz herself
jokes.punchline: Liz dismisses this as merely an opinion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz denial mechanism - treating objective gross reality as subjective opinion

jokes.text: Sir, this is a school for deaf girls.
jokes.setup: Character bragging about success at what seems like a reunion
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're at the wrong location entirely
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock absurdist misdirection - perfect setup and unexpected reveal

jokes.text: And i definitely would have gone to my reunion. But the boat i was educated on sank.
jokes.setup: Character explaining why they didn't attend reunion
jokes.punchline: They were educated on a boat that sank
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd backstory delivered deadpan - classic 30 Rock surreal world-building

jokes.text: What if something happened to her? Something bad! Then we would have a secret that bound us together.
jokes.punchline: Immediately escalates to murder conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Dark corporate humor with perfect escalation from succession planning to murder bonding

jokes.text: Either a weather event is approaching, Or...
jokes.punchline: Trailing off while watching something ominous
jokes.explanation: Sets up visual comedy through dialogue - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: How come there ain't no puerto ricans on star trek?! They got every race and life-Form in the galaxy, Except for puerto ricans. What's up with that?
jokes.punchline: Somehow excludes Puerto Ricans specifically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy observation - absurd but oddly logical complaint about sci-fi representation

jokes.text: What is this, the local?
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to local TV programming
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction to Tracy's antics with TV industry reference

jokes.text: What's that flavor? It's dove age-Fighting acne cream.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about a flavor they're tasting
jokes.punchline: It's face cream
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with good setup/payoff structure about eating beauty products

jokes.text: Well, his french-Canadian mistress, and then me.
jokes.setup: Jack was the first person Geiss called
jokes.punchline: Actually second, after his French-Canadian mistress
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Jack's importance with specific absurd detail about mistress

jokes.text: And i couldn't have done it without... my lucky coin.
jokes.setup: Jack about to credit something important for his success
jokes.punchline: It's a superstitious lucky coin
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's hidden superstitious side contradicting his rational persona

jokes.text: I wish i had a princeton reunion right now. Wipe that smug smile off michelle obama's face.
jokes.punchline: Specifically to show up Michelle Obama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack competitiveness with timely political reference showing his petty elite rivalry

jokes.text: Hey, liz, how's the telescope?
jokes.punchline: References Liz's nerdy telescope hobby to mock her
jokes.explanation: Sets up the telescope as symbol of Liz's nerdiness and social awkwardness

jokes.text: You found a hairstyle that works for you, So long as it's not too humid.
jokes.punchline: Qualified with very specific humidity restriction
jokes.explanation: Perfect backhanded compliment with very specific, relatable caveat about hair and weather

jokes.text: whittling, jug-Blowing, Ihop monkeys
jokes.punchline: Specific rural stereotypes ending with 'IHOP monkeys'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack elitism with escalating rural stereotypes ending in absurd 'IHOP monkeys'

jokes.text: Jack, it's a suburb of philadelphia.
jokes.punchline: Liz corrects him with mundane suburban reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response deflating Jack's elaborate fantasy with boring suburban reality

jokes.text: the ugly duckling has turned into A vaguely ethnic swan.
jokes.punchline: Modified to 'vaguely ethnic swan'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of fairy tale metaphor with specific oddly qualified compliment

jokes.text: They have popcorn on the plane. I want to go to there.
jokes.punchline: Liz's childlike excitement and grammatical error
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz catchphrase moment - childlike food excitement with signature grammar error

jokes.text: A beam of energy came and spoke to me During my coma. I don't know what it was. It could have been an alien. Maybe it was god. Maybe it was an unborn aztec king.
jokes.setup: Geiss explaining his decision to stay as CEO
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre spiritual visitation options
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity from alien to god to unborn Aztec king - perfectly weird 30 Rock mysticism

jokes.text: Grizz had to go to the optometrist. Making up words won't save you.
jokes.punchline: Tracy thinks 'optometrist' is a made-up word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy ignorance moment - common medical term dismissed as fictional with confidence

jokes.text: So the standards department is telling me You can only say "cat anus" twice During the show, but i'm gonna fight for you. You can say it three times. Cat anus, cat anus, cat anus!
jokes.setup: Jack discussing TV standards and practices
jokes.punchline: Fighting for the right to say 'cat anus' three times, then demonstrating
jokes.explanation: Absurd broadcast standards comedy with escalation and Jack's misplaced executive priorities

jokes.text: First william f. Buckley dies, now this. Next stop impotence, right?
jokes.setup: Liz commenting on Jack's bad year
jokes.punchline: Equating conservative intellectual's death with Jack's career setback, escalating to impotence
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity linking intellectual death to career disappointment to sexual dysfunction

jokes.text: Why does anybody go to miami? Ass and the burgeoning art scene.
jokes.setup: Liz asking why Jack is going to Miami
jokes.punchline: Crude reason paired with pretentious cultural justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack duality - base desires dressed up with cultural sophistication

jokes.text: One time i laughed at a blind guy eating spaghetti! Sometimes i pee in the shower if i'm really tired! I saw my grandparents making love once, And i didn't leave right away!
jokes.punchline: Three escalating personal confessions of increasing awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating confession structure with perfect increasing grossness and specificity

jokes.text: We have a lovely n.C. Wyeth museum. Actually, some meth addicts burned that place down Last august.
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercutting with destruction by meth addicts
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from cultured tourism to drug-fueled destruction in small town

jokes.text: the vietnamese immigrants took over All of those places. I wouldn't recommend going to little hanoi after dark Unless you're wearing a khan dong.
jokes.punchline: Treating Vietnamese community as requiring special protective clothing
jokes.explanation: Attempts absurdist small-town xenophobia but relies too heavily on ethnic stereotyping

jokes.text: We've been a dry county since 1880. It's the one tradition that remains.
jokes.punchline: Prohibition as the sole surviving tradition
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about cultural preservation focusing on restriction

jokes.text: But do you like methodist churches Full of vietnamese?
jokes.punchline: Combining religious institution with ethnic specification
jokes.explanation: Continues absurdist small-town character but lacks strong comedic structure

jokes.text: A ghetto mating call.
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's innocent misunderstanding of urban terminology
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth innocent worldview moment with economical phrasing

jokes.text: Jeesh, buy a guy a drink first.
jokes.setup: Reacting to something forward or aggressive
jokes.punchline: Treating it as romantic/sexual approach
jokes.explanation: Generic sexual innuendo response - predictable and lacks character specificity

jokes.text: This place is bigger than my apartment.
jokes.punchline: Reveals her apartment is smaller than hotel room
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Liz's modest NYC living situation

jokes.text: To get there, you just go Straight down ngo dinh diem boulevard Until you see a sign that says "detour." Now ignore that. It's a trap.
jokes.punchline: Vietnamese street name and treating detour sign as trap
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of small-town paranoia with specific historical reference

jokes.text: But cocoon, and then flap, flap, flap--Butterfly.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her transformation since high school
jokes.punchline: Physical demonstration of butterfly metamorphosis
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment with Liz awkwardly acting out transformation metaphor

jokes.text: I don't know, kelsey. How's your mom's pill addiction?
jokes.setup: Kelsey asking about telescope in mocking way
jokes.punchline: Liz escalates with dark family dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from mild teasing to dark personal attack - shows Liz's mean streak

jokes.text: We called you the white haven witch.
jokes.setup: Revealing how classmates really saw Liz
jokes.punchline: Specific mean nickname with alliteration
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of Liz's true high school reputation with memorable alliterative nickname

jokes.text: This is a beauty mark. But you thought it was funny to say that god pooped on me.
jokes.punchline: Reveals Liz's specific cruel comment from high school
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cruel and specific teenage insult that reveals Liz's mean creativity

jokes.text: You brought it back!
jokes.punchline: Kelsey's facial tic returning from high school trauma
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy showing lasting trauma from Liz's bullying manifesting physically

jokes.text: Well, i got a boat, good friends, and a trampoline. You tell me.
jokes.punchline: Lists simple pleasures ending with trampoline
jokes.explanation: Perfect simple happiness with trampoline as unexpectedly delightful detail

jokes.text: This is mostly spit.
jokes.punchline: Realizing the drink is mostly saliva
jokes.explanation: Simple gross-out humor with good economy but predictable setup

jokes.text: Still think i am "gayer than the volleyball scene In top gun"?
jokes.setup: Rob confronting Liz about past comment
jokes.punchline: Specific pop culture reference used as insult
jokes.explanation: Good specific reference showing Liz's cruel wit in high school with apt Top Gun comparison

jokes.text: I'd like you to meet my wife, With whom i've raised three beautiful dogs.
jokes.punchline: They've raised dogs instead of children, phrased like children
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay treating pets as children with formal phrasing creating humor

jokes.text: I'm so mad, all i can do is dance.
jokes.setup: Liz expressing anger about reunion situation
jokes.punchline: Anger manifesting as uncontrollable dancing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz absurdist emotional response - anger expressing itself as dance

jokes.text: Beers, boats, and buds. Doesn't that sound great?
jokes.setup: Jack trying to embrace simple pleasures
jokes.punchline: Awkward alliterative summary of working-class happiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack awkwardly trying to relate to common people with forced slogan

jokes.text: from having too much sex in it!
jokes.setup: Explaining why his Camaro broke down
jokes.punchline: Excessive sexual activity as cause of mechanical failure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from nostalgic car talk to absurd sexual bragging

jokes.text: Lemon, rich 50 is middle-Class 38, okay?
jokes.setup: Liz questioning how Jack fits in with younger crowd
jokes.punchline: Wealth-based age calculation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack logic about wealth translating to youth - perfectly quotable and character-specific

jokes.text: If we didn't exist, how would people know who to vote for?
jokes.punchline: Political guidance as primary actor function
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism and misunderstanding of celebrity political influence

jokes.text: So she can put her feet in her mouth. So can i.
jokes.punchline: Jenna claiming same physical feat
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna jealousy of baby's attention with physical flexibility claim

jokes.text: Look, let's all do the diane.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to prove she's fun at the reunion
jokes.punchline: Suggesting made-up or obscure dance
jokes.explanation: Good Liz social awkwardness trying too hard with nonsensical dance suggestion

jokes.text: What is wrong with you?! Diane?
jokes.punchline: Reveals Diane is a person present, not a dance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal that 'the Diane' isn't a dance but refers to actual person - perfect misdirection

jokes.text: Next stop, kansas city.
jokes.punchline: Treating elevator like long-distance transportation
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth innocent whimsy treating office elevator as magical transport

jokes.text: Singing in the elevator. It's like a road trip to the sky!
jokes.punchline: Poetic metaphor about vertical travel
jokes.explanation: Beautiful Kenneth whimsy with perfect metaphor combining travel and elevation

jokes.text: Kenneth is a monster! We have to stop him!
jokes.punchline: Treating friendly coworker as existential threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna jealousy escalation treating Kenneth's innocent fun as villainous plot

jokes.text: Just to be clear, we're not making out. That would be social suicide.
jokes.setup: Liz and Jack in closet together
jokes.punchline: Liz clarifying no romance for social reasons
jokes.explanation: Good meta-awareness of romantic comedy tropes with character-appropriate deflection

jokes.text: Or did you make some joke about me being impotent?
jokes.setup: Jack asking if Liz offered support during crisis
jokes.punchline: Accurately calling out her earlier impotence joke
jokes.explanation: Effective callback showing Jack's awareness of Liz's defense mechanisms

jokes.text: At least i'm not 50, alone, And sitting on 2,000 business cards For a job i'm never gonna get.
jokes.setup: Liz defending herself against Jack's criticism
jokes.punchline: Brutal personal attack using private information
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Liz's cruelty - specific, personal, and devastating attack on Jack's vulnerability

jokes.text: You went to a printer, didn't you? You picked out a font. You paid extra for a rush order. It was your happy, little secret.
jokes.setup: Liz found Jack's premade CEO business cards
jokes.punchline: Detailed description of his hopeful preparation process
jokes.explanation: Devastating escalation revealing Jack's pathetic optimism through specific printing details

jokes.text: This studio hosted The kraft television theater in the 1950s, Where young writers like paddy chayefsky and rod serling First rose to prominence.
jokes.punchline: Tracy demonstrating unexpected cultural knowledge
jokes.explanation: Ironic moment showing Tracy's hidden intelligence through TV history knowledge

jokes.text: We're not really best friends. We're just good friends.
jokes.setup: Kenneth calling Tracy his best friend
jokes.punchline: Tracy's gentle but firm correction of relationship status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment managing Kenneth's expectations with characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: I want to go to there.
jokes.setup: Hearing about Outback Steakhouse gift certificate
jokes.punchline: Liz's signature grammatically incorrect enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Second use of iconic Liz catchphrase - less surprising but still character-perfect

jokes.text: We all have ways of coping. I use sex and awesomeness.
jokes.punchline: Lists sex and awesomeness as his methods
jokes.explanation: Good Jack ego moment disguised as Larry Braverman casual bragging

jokes.text: This is your son.
jokes.punchline: Reveals major life consequence Jack wasn't aware of
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation creating maximum panic for Jack's fake identity situation

jokes.text: I am not larry braverman. I repeat, i am not larry braverman! I am liz lemon's Platonic friend, jack donaghy.
jokes.setup: Jack panicking about secret son situation
jokes.punchline: Formal declaration of real identity like emergency broadcast
jokes.explanation: Brilliant panic moment with formal emergency-style identity declaration

jokes.text: You know what, suck it, you whittling ihop monkeys!
jokes.setup: Liz's final statement to reunion crowd
jokes.punchline: Uses Jack's earlier insult as her own angry exit line
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with Liz adopting Jack's elitist insult for her own angry exit

tmdb_overview: Jack manages to convince a reluctant Liz to attend her high school reunion. Meanwhile, Don Geiss awakens from his coma and shocks Jack with a declaration of who he wants to be the CEO of GE. A shaken Jack decides he needs to get away and blow off some steam, but tags along with Liz to her reunion instead due to bad weather.
insight: High-velocity absurdist escalation carries this 64-joke episode to elite territory.
